title: HPKE key overwrite not rate-limited per-key; silent clobber
description: PUBLISH_HPKE_KEY uses ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE — silently replacing any existing key. An attacker who steals or borrows a session can replace a victim's HPKE public key with their own, then intercept all subsequent sealed-sender messages. There is no audit log, no notification to the key owner, and no key version/timestamp returned to the caller. At minimum, returning the prior key's updated_at lets the owner detect unexpected changes. Consider requiring the caller to supply the previous key hash as a precondition.
